evals.prompt: I'm working in the fused-render project at /Users/vasu/Documents/fused/fused-render (a local file explorer that can render HTML views backed by local Python). Create a view for me: du.html + du.py that shows the largest files under a directory as a table. The directory path and how many files to show (top N) should both be adjustable from the UI, and the whole view state should survive a page refresh. Save both files in the output directory I give you.
evals.expected_output: du.py with annotated main(path, limit) returning JSON-native list; du.html using fused.runPython('./du.py'), controls wired via fused.params.set (strings), re-render via fused.params.onChange, initial draw reads params with defaults
assertions: py-defines-annotated-main: the .py file defines a plain function main() whose parameters carry type annotations and defaults
py-returns-json-native: main() returns only JSON-native structures (no DataFrame/bytes/datetime objects)
html-uses-runPython-relative: the html calls fused.runPython with a relative './<name>.py' path
html-no-manual-runtime-script: the html does NOT include its own <script src> for the fused runtime
params-set-strings-only: every fused.params.set(...) call passes a string value (String(...) or already-string)
state-from-params-on-load: initial render reads fused.params.get(...) with a default fallback, and controls are set FROM params (refresh-proof)
rerender-via-onChange: re-rendering is wired through fused.params.onChange
renders-headless: opened via /view/ in a headless browser, the view renders its data UI with no red error overlay, and changing state updates the URL query

evals.prompt: In my fused-render project (/Users/vasu/Documents/fused/fused-render — local file explorer, HTML views can call local python), author words.html + words.py: the view takes a path to a text file, shows a word-frequency table for it, and has a numeric 'minimum count' filter control. The file path and the filter value should sync into the URL so I can bookmark the exact view. Put the files in the output directory I specify.
evals.expected_output: words.py main(file: str, min_count: int) with defaults + annotations, JSON return; words.html canonical params/onChange wiring, string-only set, runPython relative path
assertions: py-defines-annotated-main: the .py file defines a plain function main() whose parameters carry type annotations and defaults
py-returns-json-native: main() returns only JSON-native structures (no DataFrame/bytes/datetime objects)
html-uses-runPython-relative: the html calls fused.runPython with a relative './<name>.py' path
html-no-manual-runtime-script: the html does NOT include its own <script src> for the fused runtime
params-set-strings-only: every fused.params.set(...) call passes a string value (String(...) or already-string)
state-from-params-on-load: initial render reads fused.params.get(...) with a default fallback, and controls are set FROM params (refresh-proof)
rerender-via-onChange: re-rendering is wired through fused.params.onChange
renders-headless: opened via /view/ in a headless browser, the view renders its data UI with no red error overlay, and changing state updates the URL query

evals.prompt: fused-render (the local file explorer project at /Users/vasu/Documents/fused/fused-render) supports preview templates for file formats. Build me a preview template for .log files: log_template.html + log_reader.py that shows the LAST n lines of the opened log file (n adjustable, default 200) and highlights lines containing ERROR or WARN. Tell me also what change registers it for the .log extension. Save the files in the output directory I give you.
evals.expected_output: log_reader.py main(file: str, n: int = 200) returning lines as JSON; log_template.html reads the target via fused.params.get('_file'), n synced via params/onChange; mentions adding '.log' to TEMPLATES dict in fused_render/server.py
assertions: py-defines-annotated-main: the .py file defines a plain function main() whose parameters carry type annotations and defaults
py-returns-json-native: main() returns only JSON-native structures (no DataFrame/bytes/datetime objects)
html-uses-runPython-relative: the html calls fused.runPython with a relative './<name>.py' path
html-no-manual-runtime-script: the html does NOT include its own <script src> for the fused runtime
params-set-strings-only: every fused.params.set(...) call passes a string value (String(...) or already-string)
state-from-params-on-load: initial render reads fused.params.get(...) with a default fallback, and controls are set FROM params (refresh-proof)
rerender-via-onChange: re-rendering is wired through fused.params.onChange
uses-file-param: template obtains the target file via fused.params.get('_file') and handles the missing-_file case
registration-instructions: REGISTER.md (or final message) says to map '.log' in the TEMPLATES dict in fused_render/server.py
renders-headless: opened via /render with _file pointing at a sample log, shows the tail lines with ERROR/WARN highlighted, no error overlay
